demos.text: This special Operator skips all tasks downstream of itself if you are not on the "latest" DAG run (if the wall-clock time right now is between its execution_time and the next scheduled execution_time, and it was not an externally-triggered run).
demos.reasoning: The passage uses concise, domain-specific documentation language and precisely defines the behavior of a special operator, including terminology such as “DAG run,” “execution_time,” and “externally-triggered run.” Its phrasing is somewhat awkward and closely resembles copied technical reference documentation rather than organically generated prose. Therefore, it is more likely human-written or source-derived than AI-generated.

demos.text: This peculiar Operator bypasses every downstream task unless it belongs to the “latest” DAG run; that is, wall-clock time must fall between its execution_time and the next scheduled execution_time, and the run must not have been triggered externally.
demos.reasoning: The passage uses precise technical terminology and a compact explanatory structure, but its phrasing is somewhat unnatural and generalized (“peculiar Operator,” “wall-clock time must fall between”), which is characteristic of AI-generated or heavily paraphrased technical text. There are no clear personal or stylistic markers suggesting spontaneous human writing.
